system.effect: The character has accumulated Corruption Points from exposure to Warp taint. At certain thresholds, they must roll on the Mutation Table, potentially gaining physical or mental mutations.
system.removal: Corruption Points are permanent and cannot be removed by normal means. Only the most extraordinary circumstances or divine intervention might reduce them.

description.value:
The character's body and soul have been tainted by the Warp. Corruption is tracked as a point total. At certain thresholds, the character's corruption manifests physically or mentally:

  • 0–29 Corruption Points: No significant effect.
  • 30–59 Corruption Points: The character gains a Minor Mutation or Malignancy.
  • 60–89 Corruption Points: The character gains a Severe Mutation or Malignancy.
  • 90–99 Corruption Points: The character gains a Major Mutation and is visibly tainted.
  • 100+ Corruption Points: The character is consumed by Chaos and becomes an NPC (or worse).

system.notes: Track total Corruption Points via stacks. Mutations and Malignancies are applied separately as needed.
